context: Tests whether the agent registers webhooks in the afterAuth hook, includes all three mandatory GDPR topics and APP_UNINSTALLED, correctly handles ALREADY_EXISTS errors as non-fatal, and ensures GDPR handlers always return 200.

checklist.name: afterAuth placement
checklist.description: The README or code comments state that `registerWebhooks` should be called in the `afterAuth` hook (or equivalent OAuth completion callback), not at server startup

checklist.name: GDPR: customers-data-request
checklist.description: The registration list includes the `CUSTOMERS_DATA_REQUEST` topic

checklist.name: GDPR: customers-redact
checklist.description: The registration list includes the `CUSTOMERS_REDACT` topic

checklist.name: GDPR: shop-redact
checklist.description: The registration list includes the `SHOP_REDACT` topic

checklist.name: APP_UNINSTALLED topic
checklist.description: The registration list includes the `APP_UNINSTALLED` topic

checklist.name: ALREADY_EXISTS ignored
checklist.description: The registration code explicitly checks for the 'Address for this topic has already been taken' error message (or equivalent ALREADY_EXISTS condition) and does NOT throw or fail on it

checklist.name: Real errors still thrown
checklist.description: Errors other than ALREADY_EXISTS are still propagated (thrown or otherwise surfaced) after filtering

checklist.name: GraphQL mutation used
checklist.description: Webhook subscriptions are created using the `webhookSubscriptionCreate` GraphQL mutation (not REST API)

checklist.name: GDPR handlers return 200
checklist.description: All three GDPR endpoint handlers respond with HTTP 200, even if no data processing logic is present

checklist.name: All three GDPR routes present
checklist.description: The gdpr.ts file contains route handlers for all three paths: `/webhooks/gdpr/customers-data-request`, `/webhooks/gdpr/customers-redact`, and `/webhooks/gdpr/shop-redact`

checklist.name: callbackUrl construction
checklist.description: Each webhook's callback URL is constructed from the provided `appUrl` parameter (not hardcoded)
